customer_comment: Pepper grinder stopped working after about two weeks; different cables and outlets did not help.
Charging base no longer works, but each grinder can still be charged individually.
Charging pin spring got stuck and no longer contacts the charger.
One charging prong stuck in a retracted position, so the grinder cannot charge on the base.
Worked well at first, but the charger stopped working.
Charging base sensors are faulty and stop charging one grinder.
Base stopped charging and direct charging one unit also stopped.
After three months, the grinders stopped charging even with other cords.
One unit broke, so the reviewer does not recommend it.
Salt and pepper do not grind fine enough even at the finest setting.
Salt crystals sometimes get stuck during grinding.
Peppercorn grinding is variable and may require shaking and restarting.
Pepper gets stuck a lot.
Dispenses too much salt and leaves salt or pepper in the cradle after use.
Product feels cheap, not solid, and not sturdy.
Matte finish developed permanent oil spots and blemishes.
Filling is difficult and the compartments do not hold much salt or pepper.
Front button is bumped accidentally when handling the grinder, causing mess.
Battery life gets weak quickly and requires frequent charging.
Holds a charge for a very long time and works flawlessly.
Sleek modern design, easy to use and refill, strong battery life.
Push-button operation helps users who struggle with manual grinding.
Handles salt reliably after cheaper models failed.
Support sent a replacement quickly when one grinder stopped working.
Coarseness adjustments are easy to access and offer a good range.
Quiet compared with other grinders and dispenses the right amount.
Fine grind is possible after adjusting the bottom dial.
Only downside is that it does not show the battery percentage.
Product may require included low-amp charger; phone chargers may not work.
Good product.
